Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. 15 Years of Forking 188 upvotes, 31 comments
  2. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 318 upvotes, 59 comments
  3. Interview: Nobonoko, Master of the Minimal Sequencer 34 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 198 upvotes, 42 comments
  5. 15 years, one server, 8GB RAM and 500k users – how Webminal refuses to die 120 upvotes, 25 comments
  6. About the Atmosphere 62 upvotes, 11 comments
  7. Copilot edited an ad into my PR 629 upvotes, 191 comments
  8. The RISE RISC-V Runners: free, native RISC-V CI on GitHub 135 upvotes, 37 comments
  9. Douglas Lenat's Automated Mathematician Source Code 27 upvotes, 4 comments
  10. Ninja is a small build system with a focus on speed 23 upvotes, 3 comments
  11. Hardware Image Compression 29 upvotes, 5 comments
  12. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 552 upvotes, 210 comments
  13. Philly courts will ban all smart eyeglasses starting next week 262 upvotes, 97 comments
  14. The Cognitive Dark Forest 448 upvotes, 196 comments
  15. Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman Equation: Reinforcement Learning and Diffusion Models 41 upvotes, 12 comments
  16. I use excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 68 upvotes, 27 comments
  17. The road signs that teach travellers about France 113 upvotes, 52 comments
  18. When Coupled Volcanoes Talk, These Researchers Listen 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. VHDL's Crown Jewel 68 upvotes, 29 comments
  20. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 685 upvotes, 433 comments
  21. Stripe is down 33 upvotes, 13 comments
  22. "Roadrunner": a bipedal, wheeled robot for multi-modal locomotion [video] 41 upvotes, 18 comments
  23. Eclipse GlassFish: This Isn't Your Father's GlassFish 24 upvotes, 11 comments
  24. C++26 is done: ISO C++ standards meeting Trip Report 241 upvotes, 213 comments
  25. Midnight train from GA: A view of America from the tracks as airports struggle 101 upvotes, 84 comments
  26. Coding agents could make free software matter again 199 upvotes, 186 comments
  27. Gonon: Building a Clock with No Numerals 33 upvotes, 23 comments
  28. My MacBook keyboard is broken and it's insanely expensive to fix 208 upvotes, 251 comments
  29. The First Video Game Was Just a Box in the Corner of a Bar 5 upvotes, 2 comments
  30. How A Spartan Revolutionized Baseball 3 upvotes, 1 comments

Last generated 7:00pm, 30 March 2026. Source here.
